ops.text: Focus-rail accessibility debt, both clients: (1) iOS rail tap cells guarantee only minHeight 44 (`FocusDeckView.swift:370#xs6w` — `.frame(maxWidth: .infinity, minHeight: 44)`), so at railMax=10 on a 375pt phone each cell is ~31pt wide, under the 44×44 HIG floor; web sidesteps this by letting `.focus-dots` flex-wrap onto extra rows, iOS has no wrap and jumps straight to the no-detail proportional bar past 10 steps — an adaptive railMax or wrapping rail is the fix, but it is a visible UX change nobody has designed. (2) The muted 4pt tick (`--border-strong`/`BlockPalette.borderStrong`) and the hollow amber undecided border both sit under the WCAG 3:1 non-text contrast threshold (~1.8:1 light / ~2.8:1 dark for borderStrong) on BOTH clients — deliberate "muted" styling that a future accessibility pass should revisit with a token tweak rather than per-client hacks. Neither was introduced by the tick change (the tick replaced an even-lower-contrast chipBg fill; the cell geometry predates it); VoiceOver labeling of untallied ticks WAS a real gap and is fixed ("no decision" suffix in dotLabel).
